column1: # /Worksheet Page — insert a manual page break at the cell pointer.
# Implemented as a row insert with the literal `|::` label in column
# A: the print engine recognizes it as a section boundary, dropping
# the marker row from output and starting the next row on a new page.
# A1=Top1, A2=Top2, A3=Bot1.  DOWN commits the entry and advances.
# /WP inserts a row above Bot1 with `|::` in column A; Bot1 shifts
# down to A4.  The marker itself is non-printing.
# /WP left the pointer on the marker row (A3) — anchor at A1 before
# entering the print range.  Default pg_length is 0 (no chunking
# inside a section), so the only page boundary is the manual break.
# Form feed between the two sections — manual break honored.
